pages.title: Polaris Finance Documentation
pages.description: Polaris is the pETH-powered yield layer for all of DeFi, minting pAssets backed entirely by onchain collateral and yield. No T-bills. No CEXs. No compromises.

pages.title: Changelog
pages.description: A documentation changelog for the Polaris docs, in Keep a Changelog style. This tracks changes to the docs only, not protocol release history.

pages.title: Polaris Core Concepts
pages.description: A one-page map of the Polaris system, with every key term defined briefly and linked to its full explanation.

pages.title: Polaris Launch Status
pages.description: Polaris launch-status and on-ramp, covering official network, app URL, what's actionable today, and the basic flow from ETH to a pAsset.

pages.title: Minting Polaris pAssets
pages.description: How the Polaris CDP model mints pAssets directly from the protocol against pETH collateral, with autonomous market-aware interest rates.

pages.title: Managing a Polaris Trove
pages.description: Manage a Polaris trove around its Individual Collateralization Ratio (ICR) — add collateral, mint or repay debt, track interest, and close out cleanly.

pages.title: Open a Polaris Trove
pages.description: Step-by-step guide to opening a Polaris trove — deposit pETH, mint a pAsset, and pick a safe buffer to stay clear of liquidation and redemption.

pages.title: Polaris Asset Catalog
pages.description: The pAsset debt tokens Polaris can mint (pUSD, pGOLD, pCHF, pBigMac), how pETH differs as collateral, and how StablecoinOS can add new assets.

pages.title: pETH Bonding Curve
pages.description: How one two-way bonding curve gives pETH contract-native liquidity, a rising floor price, and protocol-wide value capture, with no liquidity providers required.

pages.title: pETH Floor Price
pages.description: pETH's floor price is a mathematically enforced minimum under the bonding-curve invariants. What it is, why it rises, and how the floor ratio frames downside.

pages.title: pETH Split: fpETH and vpETH
pages.description: How pETH splits into fpETH and vpETH, what each leg represents, how merging works, and how reserve loans use fpETH.

pages.title: POLAR Token, Staking, and Conversion
pages.description: Why POLAR exists, how it is minted only by burning pETH, why its dilution is productive, and how staking and conversion fit together.

pages.title: POLAR Conversion Auctions
pages.description: The conversion auction bridges pETH and POLAR — a spike-and-decay auction where burning pETH mints POLAR, raises the floor price, and releases ETH yield.

pages.title: POLAR Tokenomics
pages.description: How POLAR works: issuance by burning pETH, current staking, future vePOLAR, yield routes, and pending distribution terms.

pages.title: pAsset Redemptions
pages.description: A redemption swaps a below-peg pAsset for its pETH collateral at face value minus a fee. How the arbitrage restores peg, and what it means for borrowers.

pages.title: Polaris Liquidations
pages.description: A trove below its minimum collateralization ratio is liquidated to keep the pAsset backed. The Stability Pool absorbs the debt, or it redistributes to troves.

pages.title: Polaris Recovery Mode
pages.description: Recovery Mode is the protective state Polaris enters when system reserve backing runs thin relative to total debt. How it works, and how to stay safe.

pages.title: Polaris Glossary
pages.description: Every Polaris term defined in one or two sentences, alphabetized and linked to its full explanation. The fast way to look anything up.

pages.title: Polaris Audits & Security
pages.description: Polaris security model, audit-report status, bug-bounty status, and disclosure guidance. Pre-launch reports and bounty details are not published yet.

pages.title: Polaris Brand Assets
pages.description: Polaris logos, the Celestial Night Sky palette, and typography, with usage do's and don'ts. Dark mode is the canonical surface.

pages.title: Polaris Contracts & Addresses
pages.description: The Polaris contract set: a shared core plus one instance per pAsset. Addresses publish at deployment; verify network, chain ID, and source first.

pages.title: Polaris Finance FAQ
pages.description: Frequently asked questions about Polaris: what it is, how minting and troves work, how the peg holds, where yield comes from, and how security works.

pages.title: Polaris Risk Disclosure
pages.description: A comprehensive catalog of the risks of using Polaris, each paired with the mechanism that mitigates it, without implying any risk is eliminated.

pages.title: Polaris Safety and Verification
pages.description: The safety path for verifying Polaris launch status, official contracts, audit status, intended invariants, and user risk before interacting.

pages.title: Polaris Public Testnet 1 Source of Truth
pages.description: Public Public Testnet 1 source of truth: Sepolia chain ID, app URL, caveats, active pAssets, manifests, addresses, and parameters.

pages.title: Polaris Stewardship, not Governance
pages.description: Polaris keeps its core immutable and lets vePOLAR steward only bounded parameters, onboarding decisions, and edge processes that cannot be automated.

pages.title: Fee Router & Flows
pages.description: Polaris revenue routing: the Fee Router buffers protocol yield to troves and stakers, while Flows share hard-asset revenue with contracts building on Polaris.

pages.title: vePOLAR Locking and Stewardship
pages.description: vePOLAR is vote-escrowed POLAR. Lock POLAR to receive stewardship rights and a share of protocol yield without touching the immutable core.

pages.title: Dashboard & Analytics
pages.description: How to read the Polaris app Dashboard and Analytics: balances, positions, rewards, system status, protocol-wide metrics, and next actions.

pages.title: Advanced
pages.description: How to use Polaris Advanced tools for approvals, PSM actions, reserve loans, POLAR Staking, and manual transaction checks.

pages.title: Borrow
pages.description: How the Polaris Borrow tab maps to troves, CDPs, pAsset debt, reserve loans, and before-you-sign checks.

pages.title: Earn
pages.description: How the Polaris Earn tab maps to Stability Pool deposits, POLAR Staking, rewards, and first-loss risk.

pages.title: Split
pages.description: How to use the Polaris Split tab to split pETH into fpETH and vpETH, merge them back, and check transaction risk.

pages.title: Swap
pages.description: How to use the Polaris Swap tab for WETH, pETH, and pAsset swaps with quote, approval, fee, and slippage checks.

pages.title: Zap
pages.description: A guide to Polaris Zap flows from WETH to pUSD or pGOLD and into the Stability Pool, with approvals, simulation, and risk checks.

pages.title: Why Polaris Finance
pages.description: The stablecoin trilemma, the degradation pattern, and how Polaris is architected so that trustlessness and scale stop being mutually exclusive.

pages.title: Polaris Stability Pool
pages.description: The Stability Pool is a reserve of pAssets that backstops liquidations and is the primary venue for earning yield on Polaris.

pages.title: Polaris Yield Sources
pages.description: Every source of pAsset yield on Polaris is onchain, internal to the protocol, and scales with activity — yield that is owned, not rented.
